content.problemStatement:
Chef discovered that his secret recipe has been stolen. He immediately informs the police of the theft.

It is known that the policeman and thief move on the number line. You are given that:


The initial location of the policeman on the number line is XXX and his speed is 222 units per second.
The initial location of the thief on the number line is YYY and his speed is 111 unit per second.
Find the **minimum** time (in seconds) in which the policeman can catch the thief. Note that, the policeman catches the thief as soon as their locations become equal and the thief will try to evade the policeman for as long as possible.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain an integer TTT — the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two integers XXX and YYY, as described in the problem statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line the minimum time taken by the policeman to catch the thief.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two integers X and Y — the initial positions of the policeman and the thief respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum time (in seconds) required for the policeman to catch the thief.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: |1-3| = 2. Test case 2: |2-1| = 1. Test case 3: |1-1| = 0.

solution.approach: If the policeman is to the left of the thief (X < Y), both will move to the right. The relative speed is (2 - 1) = 1 unit/s, so the catch time equals the initial distance Y - X. Similarly, if X > Y both move left and the time equals X - Y. When X == Y the time is 0. Hence the answer for each test case is simply |X - Y|. This is computed in O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) — constant work for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — only a few variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0])
    idx = 1
    out = []
    for _ in range(t):
        x = int(data[idx]); y = int(data[idx+1]); idx += 2
        out.append(str(abs(x - y)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
